content.text:
I'll design a powder diffraction beamline based on your requirements. Let me reason through the optics layout before generating the JSON.

---

## Beamline Design Rationale

**Source:** U2.5 undulator (21 mm period, 219 periods = 4.6 m long) at 6 GeV — well suited for hard X-rays (8–30 keV range typical for powder diffraction).

**Energy resolution ~0.1% (ΔE/E ~ 10⁻³):** A double-crystal monochromator (DCM) with Si(111) delivers ΔE/E ~ 1.4×10⁻⁴, satisfying the requirement comfortably. We model it as two sequential **Plane Crystal** elements.

**Focusing to ~20 μm (H) × 10 μm (V):** We use a Kirkpatrick-Baez (KB) mirror pair near the end of the beamline:
- A **Toroidal Mirror** (or two separate curved mirrors) — here modelled as two **Elliptical Mirrors** in KB geometry (one focusing vertically, one horizontally), placed at ~40 m and ~41 m.

**Layout (45 m total):**

| Element | Position (m) | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Undulator source | 0 | U2.5, 4.6 m long |
| Primary slits | 14 | Define beam / reduce heatload |
| DCM crystal 1 (Si 111) | 20 | Monochromatize |
| DCM crystal 2 (Si 111) | 20.01 | Exit beam parallel, offset |
| Secondary slits | 28 | Clean up harmonics / scatter |
| KB mirror V (Elliptical) | 40 | Focus vertically (10 μm) |
| KB mirror H (Elliptical) | 41 | Focus horizontally (20 μm) |
| Sample / Screen | 45 | Powder diffractometer |

---

---

## Design Summary

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| **Source** | U2.5 undulator, 6 GeV, 4.599 m long |
| **Design energy** | 12 keV (tunable 8–30 keV by adjusting K) |
| **Monochromator** | Si(111) DCM at 20 m — ΔE/E ≈ 1.4×10⁻⁴ (< 0.1%) |
| **KB V-mirror** | Elliptical, 40 m, grazing 3 mrad, p=40 m, q=5 m → 10 µm V |
| **KB H-mirror** | Elliptical, 41 m, grazing 3 mrad, p=41 m, q=4 m → 20 µm H |
| **Total length** | 45 m |
| **Focus** | ~20 µm (H) × ~10 µm (V) FWHM |

**Key design choices:**
- **Si(111) DCM** is the standard choice for powder diffraction — intrinsic resolution far exceeds the 0.1% target, enabling high angular resolution in diffraction patterns
- **KB geometry** decouples H/V focusing, allowing independent tuning of the asymmetric focal spot
- **3 mrad grazing angle** on KB mirrors provides good reflectivity across the full tunable range with Rh or Pt coating
- Primary slits at 14 m define the coherent fraction and protect the monochromator from full-power white beam halo; secondary slits at 28 m clean up scatter and residual harmonics
